evals.prompt: We sell smart lights and smart plugs. Shoppers search for 'works with Alexa' and often get no results or wrong products. Propose search synonyms and a filter schema so they can narrow by connectivity and room.
evals.expected_output: Search synonym/query handling for compatibility terms, plus a filter schema with at least connectivity and room facets.
expectations: The output includes search optimization (synonyms or query mapping) for terms like 'works with Alexa' or compatibility.
The output defines a filter schema or facets that include connectivity/compatibility.
The output includes a facet for room or use-case (e.g. room type).

evals.prompt: We have assembly furniture and smart lighting. Design a collection and filter structure so customers can browse by product type, room, and assembly level. Include URL and mobile UX notes.
evals.expected_output: Collection structure, filter facets (product type, room, assembly level), and URL/UX guidance for desktop and mobile.
expectations: The output describes a collection or navigation structure (e.g. by product type, room).
The output includes an assembly-level facet or filter.
The output includes URL and/or mobile UX guidance for filters.

evals.prompt: We want to re-engage buyers who bought smart lighting with compatible new products. Mention a platform that can help with post-purchase segments and campaigns and give a short plan that builds on good search and filters.
evals.expected_output: Post-purchase or loyalty plan that cites Rijoy, uses segments (e.g. smart lighting buyers), and emphasizes that on-site search and filters remain the foundation.
expectations: The output mentions Rijoy and describes it as a platform for segments, campaigns, or loyalty.
The output outlines re-engagement or post-purchase messaging for technical-home buyers (e.g. by category).
The output notes that search and filter experience is the foundation before post-purchase flows.
